rulings.Case Title: IN THE MATTER OF THE GRADY LOVETT 2011 REVOCABLE TRUST
rulings.Calendar Matter: Petition To Establish The Equitable Adoption Of Princess Green By Grady Lovett
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 07/13/2020. The following issues must be addressed: 1) No courtesy copies of the attorney's declaration and memorandum of points and authorities have been provided. 2) No proposed orders have been provided. 3) According to the proof of service (filed 02/10/2020), notice of the hearing (Judicial Council form DE-120) was not served. Also, some beneficiaries entitled to specific cash gifts from the trust were not listed in the proof of service. 4) An explanation is needed as to why the issue of the parent-child relationship based on the equitable adoption doctrine for purposes of property taxation cannot be adjudicated by the San Francisco Assessment Appeals Board without this Court's judicial predetermination. *Parties and attorneys must familiarize themselves with the 4th Amended General Order of the Probate and Mental Health Division which took effect on 05/28/2020 for the court's most updated pandemic-related requirements, procedures, and protocols. The petitioning party must inform all counsel, self-represented parties and all parties entitled to notice of the re-set hearing date (if applicable), the CourtCall procedure and Court Call's phone number (1-888-882-6878).

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF DOROTHY EDA DATI
rulings.Calendar Matter: ORDER OF STATUS HEARING
rulings.Rulings: An appearance by the attorney of record is required. *A remote appearance by telephone is strongly encouraged. Parties and attorneys may arrange for a telephonic appearance by calling CourtCall at 1-888-882-6878 and obtaining an appearance access code for the hearing's scheduled date and time. The requirement for advance permission to appear by CourtCall is suspended until further notice. A CourtCall appearance may be made by mobile phone. If counsel or any party appears in person, he or she should anticipate delays at the entrance to the courthouse and inside the courthouse. Masks must be worn at all times while in the courthouse and social distancing protocols will be strictly enforced, including inside the courtroom. **Parties and attorneys must familiarize themselves with the 5th Amended General Order of the Probate and Mental Health Division which took effect on 06/17/2020 for the court's most updated pandemic-related requirements, procedures, and protocols.

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF MICHAEL SHIU KIN NG
rulings.Calendar Matter: PETITION FOR LETTERS OF ADMINISTRATION, APPOINTMENT UNDER IAEA
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 07/13/2020. The following issues must be resolved: 1) No Declaration of Real Property has been filed by the contestant petitioner. See LRSF 14.27C. 2) No courtesy copies of the documents filed by the contestant petitioner have been provided. 3) No proposed orders have been provided by the contestant petitioner. 4) No proof of publication has been filed by the contestant petitioner. 5) No declination signed by the decedent's surviving spouse who has priority for appointment over the contestant petitioner has been filed. See LRSF 14.33. 6) Item 3d(3) of the competing petition refers to "50%" of personal property. Does this reflect the decedent's 50% tenancy in common interest as his separate property or his 50% share of community property? 7) Notice of the hearing on the competing petition must be served on the alternate executor named in the contested will. See Probate Code section 8110(b). A supplemental proof of service must be filed. 8) A summons has not been served on all the parties entitled to notice under Probate Code section 8110. See Probate Code section 8250(a). 9) The name listed in item 2 of the proof of service of the summons (filed 03/16/2020) does not match the name listed on the first page. 10) The decedent's name was incorrectly spelled in the caption of the competing petition. *Parties and attorneys must familiarize themselves with the 4th Amended General Order of the Probate and Mental Health Division which took effect on 05/28/2020 for the court's most updated pandemic-related requirements, procedures, and protocols. The petitioning party must inform all counsel, self-represented parties and all parties entitled to notice of the re-set hearing date (if applicable), the CourtCall procedure and Court Call's phone number (1-888-882-6878).
